data.title: GDP Result Provides Bank of Canada Comfort in Responding to Inflation Threat — Market Talk
data.description: The decent GDP data for January may give the Bank of Canada more leeway to respond to broad-based price gains stemming from higher energy prices, says Bradley Saunders of Capital Economics. The economist says January's 0.1% gain in GDP from the previous month, alongside an estimated 0.2% increase in February, would put Canada's economy on track for 1Q growth of near 1.8% annualized. That's what the BOC is presently predicting. "This does not make rate hikes a certainty this year," Saunders says. "But any signs of price pressures broadening are now more likely to be met with policy tightening." Traders in the overnight-index swap market anticipate up to 3 quarter-point rate increases from the BOC this year. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

data.title: Royal Gold issues 2026 guidance, 5-year GEO outlook, $125M debt repayment
data.description:
Royal Gold provided 2026 sales, DD&A and tax guidance, a 5‑year GEO outlook and announced a $125 million credit‑facility repayment.

Key Highlights:

  • 2026 sales volume guidance: gold 290,000–320,000 oz, silver 3.0–3.5 Moz, copper 21–25 Mlb.
  • 2026 DD&A expected $339–$379 million; higher due to full year from Sandstorm/Horizon and Kansanshi.
  • Effective tax rate guidance for 2026 of 17–22%, assuming no discrete tax items or law changes.
  • Inaugural 5‑Year Outlook for total GEOs of 430,000–480,000 using $4,000/oz Au, $55/oz Ag, $5/lb Cu.
  • Further debt repayment: $125 million paid on March 12, 2026, leaving $600 million outstanding and $800 million available.

Original SEC Filing: ROYAL GOLD INC [ RGLD ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: MAIA Biotechnology reports >2-year overall survival for 8 NSCLC patients in Phase 2 THIO-101
data.description:
MAIA Biotechnology reported overall survival beyond two years for eight patients in its ongoing Phase 2 THIO-101 NSCLC trial.

Key Highlights:

  • Eight patients treated with ateganosine followed by cemiplimab showed overall survival exceeding 24 months without subsequent therapy.
  • Includes 1 patient in third-line with 33 months vs expected 5.8 months for that population.
  • Four patients in second-line had survival over 30 months vs documented 10.5 months for standard care in 2L.
  • All eight previously failed checkpoint inhibitor therapy; five patients continue to have ongoing survival follow-up.
  • THIO-101 Parts A and B treated 79 patients; Part C expansion enrolling up to 48 participants in Asia and Europe with acceptable safety to date.

Original SEC Filing: MAIA Biotechnology, Inc. [ MAIA ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
